key_themes: Aerospace spin-off in Q3 2026 unlocking conglomerate discount and pure-play value
Strong defense, aerospace backlog, and demand growth
Automation, robotics, and building automation as key growth drivers
Attractive valuation at ~22x 2026 EPS with Buy-rated analyst consensus
Margin expansion and solid 2026 EPS/revenue guidance
sources: 15+ recent X posts mentioning $HON or Honeywell (focus on 2026)
Earnings previews, TIKR.com analysis, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat
summary: Sentiment around Honeywell (HON) is positive as the market anticipates the Q3 2026 spin-off of its Aerospace business, which is widely expected to eliminate the current conglomerate discount and allow both the aerospace pure-play and remaining automation-focused company to be valued more richly by investors.[[1]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/honeywell-stock-why-its-aerospace-spin-could-be-hiding-its-true-value-in-2026)[[1]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/honeywell-stock-why-its-aerospace-spin-could-be-hiding-its-true-value-in-2026) Analysts maintain a consensus Buy rating with average 12-month price targets near $252 (roughly 8-10% upside from recent ~$232 levels), supported by a record backlog, defense production ramps, high-single-digit growth in Building Automation, and 2026 EPS guidance around $10.50. While Q1 2026 earnings (due April 23) are expected to show a modest year-over-year decline, the overall 2026 outlook, margin expansion targets, and exposure to automation/robotics megatrends underpin constructive investor views.[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/honeywell-international-inc-hon-expected-140006321.html)[[3]](https://public.com/stocks/hon/forecast-price-target)
